compiled_state.title: As a skill author, I have 5 additional skills with complete evals authored via the generator
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Author complete (non-TODO) eval configs for 5 additional skills using the generator workflow: generate scaffold, fill in llm-rubric assertions, commit.

**Why**: Demonstrates the generator-to-eval workflow end-to-end and increases eval coverage from 3/~30 to 8/~30 skills with substantive behavioral assertions.

**Scope**:
- IN: 5 new skill evals authored via generate-skill-eval.sh, each with at least one llm-rubric assertion exercising a named behavioral property
- OUT: Evals for all ~30 skills (future work), modifying the generator or guard

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, eval coverage has increased from 3/~30 skills to at least 8/~30, with each new eval containing at least one llm-rubric assertion that exercises a named behavioral property of the skill (not just "output is not empty")
  ← Satisfies: "Eval coverage increases from 3/~30 skills (baseline) to at least 8/~30 skills"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] Each new eval must exercise a named behavioral property — use the existing oscillation-check, brainstorm, and fix-bug evals as quality templates
- [Testing] Smoke test must use cheapest viable model (Haiku) to minimize API cost
